scripts.subtitle: Classic PAS opener
scripts.original_vo: Listen. If you're a business owner wiring money to Chinese suppliers, your bank is robbing you. Here's the math. You send 50K. Your supplier gets 48K. Where did the 2K go? Your bank took a 2.5% exchange rate margin. That's 1,200 dollars gone before intermediary fees even hit. So now your supplier won't start production. You lose a week. You lose your delivery window. You lose the next order. Stop. This is not a fee problem. This is a hidden margin problem. Banks bury it in the rate so you never see it. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank rates. The actual rate banks use with each other. Same 50K. Supplier gets the full amount. Same-day to Chinese accounts. If you're paying suppliers in China and you're still using a bank, you're lighting money on fire. Period. Search WorldFirst. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Cold open + compounding math
scripts.original_vo: Watch this. You wire 50K to your Chinese supplier. They receive 48K. You just lost 1,200 dollars and you didn't even feel it. Now do that twice a month. That's 2,400 a month. 28,800 a year. Over five years, your bank quietly took 144,000 dollars from your business. That's a salary. That's a warehouse. That's your kid's college fund. And it's not a fee. It's the exchange rate margin. 2.5% buried in the rate so you never see the line item. Plus intermediary fees. Plus the week of delay because your supplier won't start production until the full amount lands. Listen. WorldFirst sends the same 50K at near-interbank rates. Same-day to Chinese accounts. The full amount lands. Supplier starts production. You keep the 1,200. Every wire. Every month. Every year. Search WorldFirst. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Accusation opener
scripts.original_vo: Your bank stole 1,200 dollars from you on your last wire to China and you signed off on it. Here's how. You sent 50K. Supplier got 48K. You assumed the 2K was a fee. It wasn't. It was a 2.5% margin your bank packed into the exchange rate. No line item. No receipt. No refund. Then intermediary banks took another bite. Then your supplier sat on their hands for a week because the amount was short and they refused to start production. You missed your delivery window. You ate the loss. I'll say it. Banks have been doing this for decades because business owners don't read the rate. WorldFirst is different. Near-interbank rates. Same rate the banks use with each other. Same-day to Chinese accounts. Send 50K, supplier gets 50K. Stop signing off on theft. Search WorldFirst. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Mechanism-first reveal
scripts.original_vo: Here's the problem most business owners don't realize when they pay Chinese suppliers. The fee is not the fee. You see a 25 dollar wire fee on the bank statement. You think that's the cost. Wrong. The real cost is the exchange rate margin. Your bank doesn't give you the actual USD to CNY rate. They mark it up 2.5%. On a 50K wire, that's 1,200 dollars taken silently. You'll never see it on a statement because it's baked into the rate itself. Then intermediary banks shave another slice. Supplier receives 48K, refuses to start production, you eat a week of delay. This is how banks make billions off business owners who think they're getting a fair deal. Stop. WorldFirst uses near-interbank rates. The rate banks use with each other. Same 50K. Full amount lands same-day in China. Period. Search WorldFirst. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Direct address + problem stack
scripts.original_vo: If you wire money to China, you need to hear this. Real talk. Last time you sent 50K to a supplier, three things happened and you only saw one of them. One. Your bank charged you a wire fee. You saw that. Two. Your bank marked up the exchange rate 2.5% and pocketed 1,200 dollars silently. You didn't see that. Three. Intermediary banks chipped away another 200 or 300 bucks. You didn't see that either. Supplier received 48K instead of 50K. Refused to start production. You lost a week. You lost the order. You lost your reputation with that supplier. Stop wiring through banks for China payments. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank rates. The actual rate. Same-day delivery to Chinese accounts. Send 50K, supplier gets 50K, production starts today. If you're a business owner paying China, you need WorldFirst. Period. Search WorldFirst. Period.
